title: Fishhawk clarification request artifact (standard_v1 sibling)
description: An additive sibling of the plan artifact, produced by the plan stage when the planner's step-zero plannability/needs-direction check determines an issue lacks non-derivable facts or requires an operator decision (the ADR-040 FACTS/DECISIONS bucket test). Selected over plan-standard-v1 by the top-level 'kind' discriminator BEFORE validation, so plan-standard-v1.schema.json stays frozen (additionalProperties:false). The stage parks at the awaiting_input gate instead of producing a guessed plan; operator answers, keyed by question id, resume planning in the same run. Each parked question must carry a recommended default and trade-offs (the planner-prompt calibration guard) so the operator can answer or accept the default at a glance.

kind.description: Top-level discriminator. The plan package routes an artifact carrying this value to the clarification-request schema; any other artifact (carrying plan_version) is validated against plan-standard-v1. This sibling intentionally omits plan_version so the two artifacts never collide on a shared required field.

summary.description: Human-readable explanation of why the issue is not yet plannable. Rendered as the lead paragraph of the awaiting_input ping comment.

questions.description: The parked questions the operator must answer (or accept the default for) before planning can resume. Question ids must be unique within the array — answers are keyed by id, so duplicates are ambiguous. JSON Schema cannot express cross-item uniqueness on a sub-field; the runner and backend validators enforce it semantically.

type.description: v0 closed set; v0.x adds linear_ticket and jira_ticket. Mirrors plan-standard-v1.

url.description: Canonical URL of the originating ticket.

agent.description: Agent identifier matching the workflow spec's executor.agent.

version.description: Optional. Model version or build SHA when agent reports it.

timestamp.description: RFC 3339 timestamp of generation. The runner wall-clock at agent invocation.

id.description: Stable, unique key for this question. Operator answers are matched back to questions by id on resume, so it must be unique within questions and survive a round-trip through the binding-conditions channel. Lowercase kebab/snake recommended (e.g. "auth-backend").

question.description: The decision or missing fact stated as a direct question to the operator.

what_i_can_infer.description: Optional. What the agent already established from the codebase or issue, narrowing the question to the genuinely non-derivable part. Helps the operator confirm the gap is real (a question fully answerable from here is a prompt bug).

recommended_default.description: The option the agent would take absent an answer. Required by the calibration guard: parking without a recommended default is not allowed — the operator must be able to accept the default at a glance.

tradeoffs.description: The consequences of the recommended default versus the alternatives, so the operator can decide whether the default is acceptable or override it.
